content.json:
# Defender for Cloud — Security Coverage

A single view that answers: **"How well is the cloud estate protected?"**

Use the tabs below to drill in:

| Tab | Question it answers |
|---|---|
| **Executive Summary** | What's the headline? Secure Score, blind-spot subs, top KPIs, and top recommendations |
| **Secure Score per Subscription** | How does each subscription score, and where should we focus? |
| **Protection Gaps** | How many resources are unprotected per workload type? |
| **Plan Status per Subscription** | Which Defender plans are on or off in each subscription? |
| **Extensions & Features** | Are advanced capabilities (agentless scanning, malware detection, etc.) enabled? |
| **Regulatory Compliance** | Which standards are failing, and where are the governance gaps? |

---

## At a glance — security coverage

The tiles below summarize the headline numbers. Everything refreshes when you change the subscription filter at the top.

- **Secure Score** — Microsoft's average posture score across selected subscriptions (0–100%). Higher is better.
- **Subs with NO Defender plan** — subscriptions where every workload is on Free tier (full blind spot).
- **Subs with full coverage** — subscriptions where all 14 Defender plans are enabled.
- **Total subscriptions in scope** — the denominator for the numbers above.

Scroll down for the **Top 10 Recommendations** — the highest-impact unhealthy Defender recommendations across the selected subscriptions. Use the **Recommendation Severity** filter above to show High, Medium, or both.

### Version Control

- **2026-05-07** — **v1.1** — Renamed top risks to top recommendations and added severity filtering (Rhesa Baar)
- **2026-05-01** — **v1.0** — Initial release created by Rhesa Baar

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/securescores"
| where name =~ "ascScore"
| where tolower(id) == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subscriptionId, "/providers/microsoft.security/securescores/ascscore"))
| summarize arg_min(id, *) by subscriptionId
| extend pct = min_of(todouble(properties.score.percentage) * 100, 100.0)
| summarize Score = round(avg(pct), 0)
| project Metric = "Secure Score (avg %)", Value = tostring(toint(coalesce(Score, todouble(0))))

securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| summarize Cnt = dcount(subId)
| project Metric = "Total subscriptions in scope", Value = tostring(Cnt)

securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| summarize StandardPlans = countif(pricingTier == "Standard") by subId
| summarize Cnt = countif(StandardPlans == 0)
| project Metric = "Subs with NO Defender plan", Value = tostring(Cnt)

securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where planName in~ ("CloudPosture", "VirtualMachines", "AppServices", "SqlServers", "OpenSourceRelationalDatabases", "CosmosDbs", "StorageAccounts", "Containers", "KeyVaults", "Arm", "Api", "AI", "SqlServerVirtualMachines", "Dns")
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| summarize StandardPlans = countif(pricingTier == "Standard"), TotalPlans = 14 by subId
| summarize Cnt = countif(StandardPlans == 14)
| project Metric = "Subs with full Defender coverage", Value = tostring(Cnt)

content.json:
---
### Subscriptions with no Defender plans

These subscriptions have every Defender plan set to Free — meaning **zero MDC coverage**. Could be intentional (sandbox/dev) or an accidental gap. Worth a conversation with the owners.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend tier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| summarize StandardPlans = countif(tier == "Standard"),
            TotalPlansEvaluated = count() by subscriptionId
| where StandardPlans == 0
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription, TotalPlansEvaluated, StandardPlans
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Subscriptions with NO Defender plans enabled

content.json:
---
### Top 10 recommendations

These are the highest-impact Defender for Cloud recommendations that are currently **unhealthy** across your selected subscriptions. The list focuses on built-in assessments and sorts by the number of affected resources so the top rows represent the largest current exposure. Use the **Recommendation Severity** filter above to show High, Medium, or both.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/assessments"
| extend severity = tostring(properties.metadata.severity)
| extend assessmentType = tostring(properties.metadata.assessmentType)
| extend statusCode = tostring(properties.status.code)
| extend displayName = tostring(properties.displayName)
| extend category = tostring(properties.metadata.categories[0])
| where statusCode =~ "Unhealthy"
| where assessmentType =~ "BuiltIn"
| where severity in~ ({Severity})
| summarize AffectedResources = count(), Subscriptions = dcount(subscriptionId) by displayName, severity, category
| order by AffectedResources desc
| take 10
| project Recommendation = displayName, Severity = severity, Category = category, AffectedResources, Subscriptions
content.title: Top 10 recommendations by affected resources

content.json:
## Secure Score per subscription

Microsoft's Secure Score is a 0–100% posture score per subscription. It reflects how many security recommendations are healthy versus unhealthy — weighted by impact.

**How to read this:**
- **Score %** — overall posture, higher is better. Red = below 50%, green = above 80%.
- **Healthy / Unhealthy** — how many security controls passed vs failed.
- **Max points** — the maximum possible score for the subscription.

Start with the lowest scoring subscriptions — that's where the biggest posture wins are.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/securescores"
| where name =~ "ascScore"
| where tolower(id) == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subscriptionId, "/providers/microsoft.security/securescores/ascscore"))
| summarize arg_min(id, *) by subscriptionId
| extend ScorePct = round(min_of(todouble(properties.score.percentage) * 100, 100.0), 0),
         Current = todouble(properties.score.current),
         Max = todouble(properties.score.max),
         HealthyControls = toint(properties.healthyResourceCount),
         UnhealthyControls = toint(properties.unhealthyResourceCount),
         NotApplicable = toint(properties.notApplicableResourceCount)
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription,
          ScorePct,
          Current = round(Current, 1),
          MaxPoints = round(Max, 1),
          Healthy = HealthyControls,
          Unhealthy = UnhealthyControls,
          NotApplicable
| order by ScorePct asc
content.title: Secure Score per subscription

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/securescores"
| where name =~ "ascScore"
| where tolower(id) == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subscriptionId, "/providers/microsoft.security/securescores/ascscore"))
| summarize arg_min(id, *) by subscriptionId
| extend ScorePct = round(min_of(todouble(properties.score.percentage) * 100, 100.0), 0)
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription, ScorePct
| order by ScorePct asc
content.title: Score % per subscription (lowest first)

content.json:
## Where are the gaps?

This table shows the actual number of resources per Defender plan — split into what's protected and what's not.

If the **Unprotected** column shows a number above zero, those resources have no Defender coverage. They won't generate security alerts, vulnerability findings, or threat detections.

**How to read this:**
- **Total** = all resources of this type in your selected subscriptions
- **Protected** = resources in subscriptions where the plan is turned on
- **Unprotected** = resources in subscriptions where the plan is turned off — your blind spots
- **Stopped** = Azure VMs that are deallocated (NOT currently allocated/powered-off; included in Total; shown separately for context)
- **Coverage %** = Protected / Total
- **Resource Manager note** = this plan is subscription-scoped, so Total/Protected/Unprotected are subscription counts (not resource counts)

content.query:
securityresources
| where type == "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend planName = tostring(name),
         pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier),
         subId = tostring(subscriptionId),
         pricingId = tolower(tostring(id))
| where planName in~ ("CloudPosture", "VirtualMachines", "AppServices", "SqlServers", "OpenSourceRelationalDatabases", "CosmosDbs", "StorageAccounts", "Containers", "AI", "KeyVaults", "Arm", "Api")
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| project subId, planName, pricingTier
| join kind=leftouter (
    resources
    | extend vmPowerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.code)
    | extend isStopped = iff(type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines" and tolower(vmPowerState) contains "deallocated", tolong(1), tolong(0))
    | extend planCategory = case(
        type in~ ("microsoft.compute/virtualmachines", "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines"), "VirtualMachines",
        type =~ "microsoft.web/sites", "AppServices",
        type in~ ("microsoft.sql/servers", "microsoft.sql/managedinstances", "microsoft.dbforpostgresql/flexibleservers", "microsoft.dbformysql/flexibleservers", "microsoft.dbformariadb/servers"), "SqlServers",
        type =~ "microsoft.documentdb/databaseaccounts", "CosmosDbs",
        type =~ "microsoft.storage/storageaccounts", "StorageAccounts",
        type in~ ("microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters", "microsoft.containerregistry/registries"), "Containers",
        type in~ ("microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts", "microsoft.machinelearningservices/workspaces"), "AI",
        type =~ "microsoft.keyvault/vaults", "KeyVaults",
        type =~ "microsoft.apimanagement/service", "Api",
        ""
    )
    | where planCategory != ""
    | summarize ResourceCount = count(), StoppedCount = sum(isStopped) by subscriptionId, planCategory
    | union (
        resourcecontainers
        | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
        | extend planCategories = dynamic(["CloudPosture", "Arm"])
        | mv-expand planCategory = planCategories to typeof(string)
        | summarize ResourceCount = count(), StoppedCount = tolong(0) by subscriptionId, planCategory
    )
) on $left.subId == $right.subscriptionId and $left.planName == $right.planCategory
| extend ResourceCount = coalesce(ResourceCount, tolong(0))
| extend StoppedCount = coalesce(StoppedCount, tolong(0))
| summarize
    TotalResources = sum(ResourceCount),
    Protected = sumif(ResourceCount, pricingTier == "Standard"),
    Unprotected = sumif(ResourceCount, pricingTier == "Free"),
    Stopped = sum(StoppedCount)
    by planName
| extend DisplayName = case(
    planName =~ "CloudPosture", "DCSPM (per subscription basis)",
    planName =~ "VirtualMachines", "Servers (VMs & Arc)",
    planName =~ "AppServices", "App Services",
    planName =~ "SqlServers", "Databases (SQL & OSS)",
    planName =~ "OpenSourceRelationalDatabases", "Databases (SQL & OSS)",
    planName =~ "CosmosDbs", "Cosmos DB",
    planName =~ "StorageAccounts", "Storage Accounts",
    planName =~ "Containers", "Containers (AKS & ACR)",
    planName =~ "AI", "AI Services",
    planName =~ "KeyVaults", "Key Vault",
    planName =~ "Arm", "Resource Manager (per subscription basis)",
    planName =~ "Api", "API Management",
    planName
)
| summarize
    TotalResources = sum(TotalResources),
    Protected = sum(Protected),
    Unprotected = sum(Unprotected),
    Stopped = sum(Stopped)
    by DisplayName
| extend CoveragePct = iff(TotalResources > 0, round(todouble(Protected) / todouble(TotalResources) * 100, 0), todouble(-1))
| order by Unprotected desc
content.title: Protection gaps per resource type

content.query:
securityresources
| where type == "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend planName = tostring(name),
         pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier),
         subId = tostring(subscriptionId),
         pricingId = tolower(tostring(id))
| where planName in~ ("CloudPosture", "VirtualMachines", "AppServices", "SqlServers", "CosmosDbs", "StorageAccounts", "Containers", "AI", "KeyVaults", "Api")
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| project subId, planName, pricingTier
| join kind=leftouter (
    resources
    | extend vmPowerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.code)
    | extend isStopped = iff(type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines" and tolower(vmPowerState) contains "deallocated", tolong(1), tolong(0))
    | extend planCategory = case(
        type in~ ("microsoft.compute/virtualmachines", "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines"), "VirtualMachines",
        type =~ "microsoft.web/sites", "AppServices",
        type in~ ("microsoft.sql/servers", "microsoft.sql/managedinstances", "microsoft.dbforpostgresql/flexibleservers", "microsoft.dbformysql/flexibleservers", "microsoft.dbformariadb/servers"), "SqlServers",
        type =~ "microsoft.documentdb/databaseaccounts", "CosmosDbs",
        type =~ "microsoft.storage/storageaccounts", "StorageAccounts",
        type in~ ("microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters", "microsoft.containerregistry/registries"), "Containers",
        type in~ ("microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts", "microsoft.machinelearningservices/workspaces"), "AI",
        type =~ "microsoft.keyvault/vaults", "KeyVaults",
        type =~ "microsoft.apimanagement/service", "Api",
        ""
    )
    | where planCategory != ""
    | summarize ResourceCount = count(), StoppedCount = sum(isStopped) by subscriptionId, planCategory
    | union (
        resourcecontainers
        | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
        | project subscriptionId, planCategory = "CloudPosture", ResourceCount = tolong(1), StoppedCount = tolong(0)
    )
) on $left.subId == $right.subscriptionId and $left.planName == $right.planCategory
| extend ResourceCount = coalesce(ResourceCount, tolong(0))
| extend StoppedCount = coalesce(StoppedCount, tolong(0))
| summarize
    Protected = sumif(ResourceCount, pricingTier == "Standard"),
    Unprotected = sumif(ResourceCount, pricingTier == "Free"),
    Stopped = sum(StoppedCount)
    by planName
| extend DisplayName = case(
    planName =~ "CloudPosture", "DCSPM (per subscription basis)",
    planName =~ "VirtualMachines", "Servers",
    planName =~ "AppServices", "App Svc",
    planName =~ "SqlServers", "Databases",
    planName =~ "CosmosDbs", "Cosmos",
    planName =~ "StorageAccounts", "Storage",
    planName =~ "Containers", "Containers",
    planName =~ "AI", "AI",
    planName =~ "KeyVaults", "Key Vault",
    planName =~ "Api", "APIM",
    planName
)
| project DisplayName, Protected, Unprotected, Stopped
content.title: At a glance: green = covered, red = exposed

content.json:
---
### Which subscriptions have unprotected resources?

The table below only shows subscriptions where resources exist but the corresponding Defender plan is not enabled. Sorted by the largest gap first — so you can prioritize what to address.

The **Stopped** column shows how many of those unprotected resources are deallocated (powered-off/not currently running) Azure VMs.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type == "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend planName = tostring(name),
         pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier),
         subId = tostring(subscriptionId),
         pricingId = tolower(tostring(id))
| where planName in~ ("VirtualMachines", "AppServices", "StorageAccounts", "Containers", "KeyVaults", "AI", "Api")
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| where pricingTier == "Free"
| project subId, planName
| join kind=inner (
    resources
    | extend vmPowerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.code)
    | extend isStopped = iff(type =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines" and tolower(vmPowerState) contains "deallocated", tolong(1), tolong(0))
    | extend planCategory = case(
        type in~ ("microsoft.compute/virtualmachines", "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines"), "VirtualMachines",
        type =~ "microsoft.web/sites", "AppServices",
        type =~ "microsoft.storage/storageaccounts", "StorageAccounts",
        type in~ ("microsoft.containerservice/managedclusters", "microsoft.containerregistry/registries"), "Containers",
        type =~ "microsoft.keyvault/vaults", "KeyVaults",
        type in~ ("microsoft.cognitiveservices/accounts", "microsoft.machinelearningservices/workspaces"), "AI",
        type =~ "microsoft.apimanagement/service", "Api",
        ""
    )
    | where planCategory != ""
    | summarize ResourceCount = count(), StoppedCount = sum(isStopped) by subscriptionId, planCategory
) on $left.subId == $right.subscriptionId and $left.planName == $right.planCategory
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on $left.subId == $right.subscriptionId
| extend SubscriptionName = coalesce(subscriptionName, subId)
| extend DisplayName = case(
    planName == "VirtualMachines", "Servers",
    planName == "AppServices", "App Services",
    planName == "StorageAccounts", "Storage Accounts",
    planName == "Containers", "Containers",
    planName == "KeyVaults", "Key Vault",
    planName == "AI", "AI Services",
    planName == "Api", "API Management",
    planName
)
| project SubscriptionName, DisplayName, ResourceCount, StoppedCount
| order by ResourceCount desc
content.title: Unprotected resources — per subscription

content.json:
---
### Unprotected servers — detailed list

This list shows each unprotected server resource (Azure VM and Arc machine) in subscriptions where Defender for Servers is Free. The **Power State** column shows whether each VM is running or deallocated (powered-off — not currently allocated to compute resources).

content.query:
securityresources
| where type == "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend planName = tostring(name),
         pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier),
         subId = tostring(subscriptionId),
         pricingId = tolower(tostring(id))
| where planName =~ "VirtualMachines"
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| where pricingTier == "Free"
| project subId
| join kind=inner (
    resources
    | where type in~ ("microsoft.compute/virtualmachines", "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines")
    | extend vmPowerState = tostring(properties.extended.instanceView.powerState.code)
    | project subscriptionId, resourceId = id, resourceName = name, resourceType = type, resourceGroup, location, powerState = vmPowerState
) on $left.subId == $right.subscriptionId
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on $left.subId == $right.subscriptionId
| extend SubscriptionName = coalesce(subscriptionName, subId)
| extend ResourceKind = case(
    resourceType =~ "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines", "Azure VM",
    resourceType =~ "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines", "Arc Machine",
    resourceType
)
| extend PowerState = iff(ResourceKind == "Arc Machine", "N/A", iff(isempty(powerState), "Unknown", powerState))
| project SubscriptionName, ResourceKind, resourceName, resourceGroup, location, PowerState, resourceId
| order by SubscriptionName asc, ResourceKind asc, resourceName asc
content.title: Unprotected servers — resource list

content.json:
## Plan status per subscription

This shows which Defender plans are turned on or off in each subscription. A green checkmark means the plan is active and protecting resources. A grey icon means it's off — resources in that subscription have no coverage for that workload type.

If a sub-plan is active (like Server P1 vs P2), that detail is shown in parentheses.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier), subPlan = tostring(properties.subPlan)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| extend planSet = pack(planName, case(pricingTier =~ "Free", "Off", isnotempty(subPlan), strcat("On (", subPlan, ")"), "On"))
| summarize defenderPlans = make_bag(planSet) by subscriptionId
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend SubscriptionName = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project SubscriptionName,
    CSPM = tostring(defenderPlans.CloudPosture),
    Servers = tostring(defenderPlans.VirtualMachines),
    Containers = tostring(defenderPlans.Containers),
    AppServices = tostring(defenderPlans.AppServices),
    Storage = tostring(defenderPlans.StorageAccounts),
    SQL = tostring(defenderPlans.SqlServers),
    SQL_on_VMs = tostring(defenderPlans.SqlServerVirtualMachines),
    OSS_Databases = tostring(defenderPlans.OpenSourceRelationalDatabases),
    CosmosDB = tostring(defenderPlans.CosmosDbs),
    KeyVault = tostring(defenderPlans.KeyVaults),
    ResourceManager = tostring(defenderPlans.Arm),
    APIs = tostring(defenderPlans.Api),
    AI = tostring(defenderPlans.AI),
    DNS = tostring(defenderPlans.Dns)
| order by SubscriptionName asc
content.title: Which plans are on or off?

content.json:
## Extensions & Features per Plan

Each Defender plan has optional extensions that add capabilities like agentless scanning, malware detection, or sensitive data discovery. Below you'll find a separate table per plan so it's easy to see what's on and what's off.

---
### Defender CSPM

Extensions for Cloud Security Posture Management. These enable agentless scanning of VMs and Kubernetes, container registry vulnerability assessments, sensitive data discovery, and Entra permissions insights.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings" and name =~ "CloudPosture"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| mv-expand ext = properties.extensions
| extend extName = tolower(tostring(ext.name)), extEnabled = tobool(ext.isEnabled)
| summarize AgentlessVmScanningFlag = max(case(extName contains "agentless" and (extName contains "vm" or extName contains "machine"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), AgentlessK8sDiscoveryFlag = max(case((extName contains "kubernetes" or extName contains "k8s") and (extName contains "discovery" or extName contains "api"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), ContainerRegistryVAFlag = max(case((extName contains "containerregistr" or extName contains "registry") and (extName contains "vulnerability" or extName contains "assessment"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), SensitiveDataDiscoveryFlag = max(case(extName contains "sensitive" and extName contains "data", toint(extEnabled), 0)), EntraPermissionsFlag = max(case(extName contains "entra" or extName contains "ciem" or (extName contains "permission" and extName contains "management"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), ApiSecurityPostureFlag = max(case(extName contains "api" and (extName contains "security" or extName contains "posture"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), ServerlessProtectionFlag = max(case(extName contains "serverless", toint(extEnabled), 0)) by subscriptionId, pricingTier
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription,
    Tier = pricingTier,
    AgentlessVmScanning = iff(AgentlessVmScanningFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    AgentlessK8sDiscovery = iff(AgentlessK8sDiscoveryFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    ContainerRegistryVA = iff(ContainerRegistryVAFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    SensitiveDataDiscovery = iff(SensitiveDataDiscoveryFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    EntraPermissions = iff(EntraPermissionsFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    ApiSecurityPosture = iff(ApiSecurityPostureFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    ServerlessProtection = iff(ServerlessProtectionFlag == 1, "True", "False")
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Defender CSPM extensions

content.json:
---
### Defender for Servers

Extensions for server protection. Includes MDE (Microsoft Defender for Endpoint) integration and agentless VM scanning for vulnerability assessment without installing agents.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings" and name =~ "VirtualMachines"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier), subPlan = tostring(properties.subPlan)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| mv-expand ext = properties.extensions
| extend extName = tolower(tostring(ext.name)), extEnabled = tobool(ext.isEnabled)
| summarize LogAnalyticsAgentFlag = max(case(extName contains "mmaagent" or (extName contains "log" and extName contains "analytics"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), VulnerabilityAssessmentFlag = max(case(extName contains "vulnerability" and (extName contains "assessment" or extName contains "mde"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), GuestConfigurationFlag = max(case(extName contains "guestconfiguration" or (extName contains "guest" and extName contains "configuration"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), EndpointProtectionFlag = max(case(extName contains "mdedesignatedsubscription" or extName contains "endpoint" or (extName contains "mde" and extName contains "subscription"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), AgentlessVmScanningFlag = max(case(extName contains "agentless" and (extName contains "vm" or extName contains "machine"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), FileIntegrityMonitoringFlag = max(case(extName contains "fileintegrity" or (extName contains "file" and extName contains "integrity"), toint(extEnabled), 0)) by subscriptionId, pricingTier, subPlan
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| extend Tier = case(isnotempty(subPlan), strcat(pricingTier, " (", subPlan, ")"), pricingTier)
| project Subscription,
    Tier,
    LogAnalyticsAgent = iff(LogAnalyticsAgentFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    VulnerabilityAssessment = iff(VulnerabilityAssessmentFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    GuestConfiguration = iff(GuestConfigurationFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    EndpointProtection = iff(EndpointProtectionFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    AgentlessVmScanning = iff(AgentlessVmScanningFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    FileIntegrityMonitoring = iff(FileIntegrityMonitoringFlag == 1, "True", "False")
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Defender for Servers extensions

content.json:
---
### Defender for Containers

Extensions for container workloads. Covers agentless Kubernetes discovery, container registry vulnerability scanning, and agentless VM scanning for container hosts.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings" and name =~ "Containers"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| mv-expand ext = properties.extensions
| extend extName = tolower(tostring(ext.name)), extEnabled = tobool(ext.isEnabled)
| summarize AgentlessVmScanningFlag = max(case(extName contains "agentless" and (extName contains "vm" or extName contains "machine"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), DefenderSensorFlag = max(case(extName contains "defenderdaemonset" or (extName contains "defender" and extName contains "sensor") or (extName contains "daemon" and extName contains "set"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), AzurePolicyFlag = max(case(extName contains "azurepolicy" or (extName contains "azure" and extName contains "policy"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), K8sApiAccessFlag = max(case((extName contains "kubernetes" or extName contains "k8s") and (extName contains "api" or extName contains "discovery"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), RegistryAccessFlag = max(case((extName contains "containerregistr" or extName contains "registry") and (extName contains "vulnerability" or extName contains "assessment" or extName contains "access"), toint(extEnabled), 0)) by subscriptionId, pricingTier
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription,
    Tier = pricingTier,
    AgentlessVmScanning = iff(AgentlessVmScanningFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    DefenderSensor = iff(DefenderSensorFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    AzurePolicy = iff(AzurePolicyFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    K8sApiAccess = iff(K8sApiAccessFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    RegistryAccess = iff(RegistryAccessFlag == 1, "True", "False")
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Defender for Containers extensions

content.json:
---
### Defender for Storage

Extensions for storage account protection. On-upload malware scanning checks blobs as they're written. Sensitive data discovery identifies storage accounts containing PII or other classified data.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings" and name =~ "StorageAccounts"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| mv-expand ext = properties.extensions
| extend extName = tolower(tostring(ext.name)), extEnabled = tobool(ext.isEnabled)
| summarize MalwareScanningFlag = max(case(extName contains "malware" or (extName contains "upload" and extName contains "scan"), toint(extEnabled), 0)), SensitiveDataDiscoveryFlag = max(case(extName contains "sensitive" and extName contains "data", toint(extEnabled), 0)) by subscriptionId, pricingTier
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription,
    Tier = pricingTier,
    MalwareScanning = iff(MalwareScanningFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    SensitiveDataDiscovery = iff(SensitiveDataDiscoveryFlag == 1, "True", "False")
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Defender for Storage extensions

content.json:
---
### Defender for AI

Extensions for AI workload protection. Suspicious prompt evidence captures prompt injection attempts. Data security for AI interactions monitors data shared with AI services. AI Model Security (Preview) detects vulnerabilities in deployed AI models.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings" and name =~ "AI"
| extend subId = tostring(subscriptionId), pricingId = tolower(tostring(id)), planName = tostring(name), pricingTier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| where pricingId == tolower(strcat("/subscriptions/", subId, "/providers/microsoft.security/pricings/", planName))
| extend tierRank = case(pricingTier == "Standard", 2, pricingTier == "Free", 1, 0)
| summarize arg_max(tierRank, *) by subId, planName
| mv-expand ext = properties.extensions
| extend extName = tolower(tostring(ext.name)), extEnabledRaw = tolower(tostring(coalesce(ext.isEnabled, ext.enabled, ext.state, ext.value)))
| extend extEnabledNum = iff(extEnabledRaw in ("true", "1", "yes", "on", "enabled"), 1, 0)
| summarize SuspiciousPromptEvidenceFlag = max(case((extName contains "suspicious" and extName contains "prompt") or extName contains "promptevidence", extEnabledNum, 0)), DataSecurityForAIFlag = max(case((extName contains "data" and extName contains "security") or extName contains "purview", extEnabledNum, 0)), AIModelSecurityFlag = max(case((extName contains "model" and extName contains "security") or extName contains "aimodelsecurity", extEnabledNum, 0)) by subscriptionId, pricingTier
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription,
    Tier = pricingTier,
    SuspiciousPromptEvidence = iff(SuspiciousPromptEvidenceFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    DataSecurityForAI = iff(DataSecurityForAIFlag == 1, "True", "False"),
    AIModelSecurity = iff(AIModelSecurityFlag == 1, "True", "False")
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Defender for AI extensions

content.json:
## Regulatory Compliance

This tab shows the compliance status for each standard enabled in your subscriptions. Defender for Cloud maps its security recommendations to controls in frameworks like Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark, PCI DSS, ISO 27001, NIST, SOC 2, and more.

**How to read this:**
- **Compliance %** — percentage of controls that passed. Red = low, green = high.
- **Failed** — controls where one or more linked recommendations are unhealthy.
- **Passed** — controls where all linked recommendations are healthy.
- **Skipped** — controls marked as not applicable (exempt).

> To assign additional standards, go to **Defender for Cloud > Regulatory Compliance > Manage compliance policies**.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/regulatorycompliancestandards"
| extend standardName = tostring(name)
| extend passedControls = toint(properties.passedControls)
| extend failedControls = toint(properties.failedControls)
| extend skippedControls = toint(properties.skippedControls)
| extend totalControls = passedControls + failedControls
| extend pct = iff(totalControls > 0, round(todouble(passedControls) / todouble(totalControls) * 100, 0), todouble(-1))
| extend state = tostring(properties.state)
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription, Standard = standardName, State = state, Passed = passedControls, Failed = failedControls, Skipped = skippedControls, CompliancePct = pct
| order by CompliancePct asc
content.title: Compliance by standard and subscription (lowest first)

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/regulatorycompliancestandards"
| extend standardName = tostring(name)
| extend passedControls = toint(properties.passedControls)
| extend failedControls = toint(properties.failedControls)
| extend totalControls = passedControls + failedControls
| extend pct = iff(totalControls > 0, round(todouble(passedControls) / todouble(totalControls) * 100, 0), todouble(-1))
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription, Standard = standardName, CompliancePct = pct
| order by CompliancePct asc
content.title: Compliance % by standard and subscription

content.json:
---
### Governance gap: subscriptions with no DCSPM or compliance standards

If a subscription has **Defender CSPM (CloudPosture)** set to Free, it receives only the default Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark. It will not be assessed against additional regulatory standards (PCI, ISO, SOC, etc.). The table below shows subscriptions that have zero Standard-tier plans enabled — the largest governance blind spots.

content.query:
securityresources
| where type =~ "microsoft.security/pricings"
| extend tier = tostring(properties.pricingTier)
| summarize StandardPlans = countif(tier == "Standard"),
            TotalPlansEvaluated = count() by subscriptionId
| where StandardPlans == 0
| join kind=leftouter (
    securityresources
    | where type =~ "microsoft.security/regulatorycompliancestandards"
    | extend failedControls = toint(properties.failedControls)
    | summarize Standards = dcount(name), TotalFailedControls = sum(failedControls) by subscriptionId
) on subscriptionId
| join kind=leftouter (
    resourcecontainers
    | where type =~ "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
    | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName = name
) on subscriptionId
| extend Subscription = coalesce(subscriptionName, subscriptionId)
| project Subscription, TotalPlansEvaluated, StandardPlans, ComplianceStandards = coalesce(Standards, 0), TotalFailedControls = coalesce(TotalFailedControls, 0)
| order by Subscription asc
content.title: Subscriptions with no Defender plans (governance blind spots)
